text.title: Anti-patterns to avoid
text.summary: Failure modes observed across batches; avoid each by following the cited rule.

antipattern.description: Long inline prompt text in the orchestrator's spawn message instead of a path to a `prompts/*.md` file.
antipattern.reason: Behaviour drifts run-to-run, prompts cannot be reviewed or diffed, and parallel spawns desynchronize.

antipattern.description: One subagent doing two phases at once (e.g., executing AND capturing AND delivering).
antipattern.reason: Concerns blur, errors in one phase mask another, and re-runs become impossible. Keep one phase per prompt file.

antipattern.description: Skipping the clarification batch and asking the user a fresh question every time something is uncertain.
antipattern.reason: The user's attention is finite. Fragmenting questions degrades the answer quality and erodes trust in the orchestrator's planning.

antipattern.description: Running multiple parallel executors against the main working tree instead of isolated worktrees.
antipattern.reason: Concurrent edits race on uncommitted local state and on the index. Worktrees are cheap; use them.

antipattern.description: Auto-resolving a merge conflict with `--ours` or `--theirs`.
antipattern.reason: Silently destroys one side's intent. Use the resolver subagent which reads both sides' specs and combines them.

antipattern.description: Bypassing a failing pre-commit hook with `--no-verify`.
antipattern.reason: The hook exists because the project decided that check is mandatory. Fix the underlying issue and re-commit.

antipattern.description: Full-page 4000-px-tall screenshots delivered through a chat channel.
antipattern.reason: The channel downscales the image; text becomes unreadable. The bug area is a needle in a haystack.
They convey nothing the file name does not already convey. Captions must explain the bug or the fix in plain language.

antipattern.description: Pretending a defensive fix has a visible diff by inventing a fixture without disclosing it.
antipattern.reason: The reviewer is being shown evidence under false pretences. State explicitly when a fix is defensive and any data was simulated.

antipattern.description: Pushing or deploying because the user authorized it once in a previous run.
antipattern.reason: Authorization is per-run, per-target. A blanket "yes" yesterday is not consent today.
